name: Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA), Act 4 of 2013
summary: South Africa's comprehensive data protection law regulates the processing of personal information by public and private bodies. It commenced on 1 July 2020 with a one-year grace period, making enforcement effective from 1 July 2021. POPIA grants data subjects extensive rights similar to the GDPR.
keyRequirements: Process personal information only with a lawful justification (consent, contract, legal obligation, etc.)
Implement adequate security safeguards to protect personal information
Notify the Information Regulator and data subjects of security compromises
Appoint an Information Officer and register with the Information Regulator
Conduct impact assessments for high-risk processing activities
Restrict cross-border transfers to countries with adequate protection
Ensure purpose limitation, minimality, and data quality
penalties: Administrative fines of up to ZAR 10 million or 10% of annual turnover, whichever is higher. Criminal penalties include up to 10 years imprisonment for serious offenses. Enforced by the Information Regulator (South Africa).
